title: Fluent Bit and Fluentd TLS Certificate Errors on Output Connections
app: Edge log forwarders with TLS to Splunk HEC or cloud sinks
spl:
index=fluent (sourcetype="fluentbit:log" OR sourcetype="fluentd:log")
| search "SSL" OR "TLS" OR "x509" OR "certificate" OR "handshake"
| search "error" OR "failed" OR "verify" OR "unknown ca"
| stats count, values(host) as hosts by dest_host, plugin_id
| where count >= 5
| sort -count
description: Certificate rotation and enterprise MITM appliances frequently break forwarder TLS first; agents log explicit verification errors before buffers fill. Grouping by destination host speeds collaboration with PKI teams.
value: Avoids prolonged log outages after cert renewals and supports audit of encryption posture.
implementation: Extract `dest_host` from forward plugin config via sidecar fields. Create allowlist for planned rotations. Pair with Splunk `sslPassword` rotation change tickets.
visualization: Bar chart (errors by destination), timeline, table with OpenSSL reason codes.
grandmaExplanation: We scan for security-handshake errors when sending logs. Those usually mean an expired certificate or a changed security appliance—not a mystery network glitch.
